title: Youth Residential Support Worker
description:
Are you looking for a flexible role where you can make a real impact on the lives of young people in care?

Purpose of the Job
As a Children's mainstream Residential Support Worker, you'll provide essential support to young people aged 12–17 living in mainstream residential homes. Your role is to offer stability, promote independence, and create a safe, nurturing environment where young people can thrive.

This is ad-hoc, temporary work, ideal for those seeking flexibility while still wanting to make a meaningful difference.

Key Details of the Job
Location: various locations in Leeds
Pay: £14.82 per hour + uplifts for waking nights and weekends
Contract: Ad-hoc temporary work (no guaranteed hours)
Shifts: Earlies, lates, sleep-ins, waking nights

Requirements
Ligature Training (mandatory)
Minimum 1 year experience in residential childcare, youth support, safeguarding, or a similar environment
Strong understanding of safeguarding, risk management, and de-escalation
Experience engaging positively with young people who may display challenging behaviours

Desirable
Experience in Mental Health Secure settings
Additional behavioural management or risk-reduction training

Apply Today
If you're ready to support young people and work flexibly around your lifestyle, we'd love to hear from you.
summary:
Flexible Leeds residential support role for young people in care.
Covers safeguarding, behavioural support and varied shift patterns.

title: Behaviour Support Worker
company: Veritas Education Recruitment Ltd • Agency • Contract
description:
Job: Behaviour Support Worker
Type: Long Term Contract
Start Date: April 2026
Full Time - Leeds

Veritas Education are working with an alternative provision within the Leeds area who are looking for an experienced Behaviour Support Worker to start after the Easter holidays until the end of the academic year.

The provision supports children aged 11–16 who are on temporary exclusion or at risk of permanent exclusion. It provides an environment to support academic, social and emotional development and transition back into mainstream or specialist education.

The role involves supporting key children on a 1:1 basis, helping with learning, emotional regulation and behaviour management across the setting.

The ideal candidate will have experience with SEMH needs, confidence working one-to-one, and a proactive approach to supporting engagement in lessons.

Training such as Level 2 Team Teach may be provided.
summary:
Leeds contract role supporting pupils with behavioural and SEMH needs.
Focus on 1:1 support, regulation and classroom engagement.

title: Residential Childcare Support Worker (Sessional/Casual)
description:
Residential Childcare Support Worker (Sessional/Casual)

Specific Hours: Shift pattern - ad-hoc cover
Salary: £12.29 per hour
Location: Leeds

LCS are seeking committed and enthusiastic Residential Childcare Support Workers to work on a casual basis covering shifts as required.

The role involves supporting children in residential care settings, ensuring safety, engagement and wellbeing during waking night or flexible shifts.
summary:
Casual Leeds childcare support role covering ad-hoc shifts.
Focused on residential care and flexible working patterns.

title: Residential Childcare Support Worker
description:
Contract: 35 hours per week plus sleep-in shifts

Salary: Base salary of £23,277.80 with potential to earn up to £28,893.80 with sleep-in shifts

Location: Leeds

LCS are looking for committed and enthusiastic Residential Childcare Support Workers to work full time with additional shift opportunities.

The role involves supporting young people in residential settings, promoting wellbeing, safety and development.
summary:
Full-time Leeds residential childcare role with sleep-in shifts.
Focus on supporting young people in structured care settings.

title: Support Worker
description:
Are you good with people? Would you like to support individuals into meaningful employment?

At Courtyard Services in Horsforth, Leeds, you will support people with learning disabilities to develop skills and independence.

The role includes:
- Supporting daily living activities such as cooking, cleaning and household tasks
- Supporting appointments, social outings and community activities
- Providing emotional support and promoting wellbeing
- Maintaining accurate records and safeguarding standards

This role requires previous support or care experience and a passion for helping others.
summary:
Leeds support worker role helping adults with learning disabilities build independence.
Includes daily living support and community engagement.

title: Complex Care Assistant
description:
Female Complex Care Assistant

Location: Brighouse
Pay: £14.24 per hour (including holiday pay)

Permanent contract available after successful probation.

Applicants must have the right to work in the UK, be drivers with access to a vehicle, and meet specific client care needs.

This role is restricted to female applicants under the Equality Act 2010.
summary:
Female complex care role near Brighouse requiring driving and client-specific care support.
Includes holiday pay and route to permanent contract.

title: Complex Care Assistant

Female Complex Care Assistant

summary:

title: SEN Support Workers
description:
SEN Support Workers needed across Halifax and Huddersfield.

You will support pupils with autism, ADHD, SEMH and complex learning needs.

Responsibilities include:
- 1:1 and small group support
- Supporting learning and emotional development
- Managing behaviour and routines
- Working with teaching staff and professionals

Experience with SEN or similar environments is required.
summary:
SEN support roles across Halifax and Huddersfield in education settings.
Focus on 1:1 support and behavioural management.

title: SEMH Support Worker
description:
SEMH Support Worker - Wakefield

Full-time, long-term role supporting young people with behavioural and emotional needs.

Responsibilities include:
- Building relationships and supporting behaviour
- Providing 1:1 and group support
- Promoting emotional regulation and coping strategies
- Supporting routines and de-escalation

Experience with SEMH or behavioural needs is essential.
summary:
Wakefield SEMH support role in specialist settings.
Includes behavioural support and emotional regulation work.

title: Relief Support Worker
description:
Relief Support Worker - Wakefield

Providing personalised support to adults with learning disabilities.

Responsibilities include:
- Personal care and daily living support
- Supporting community engagement and independence
- Working flexible shifts including weekends
- Maintaining accurate records and collaboration with teams

Training and development opportunities provided.
summary:
Wakefield relief support role for adults with learning disabilities.
Includes flexible shifts and development opportunities.
